description_long: This cowshed is a large, L-shaped agricultural building with a prominent red metal hip roof and beige wooden plank walls. The structure features an open bay on one end, supported by sturdy wooden posts, likely serving as a sheltered area for livestock or equipment. The main section includes two large barn-style doors, suggesting access for animals or vehicles, while the side extension appears fully enclosed. The roof overhangs provide additional weather protection, and the overall construction is robust, suitable for rural or peri-urban environments. No windows are visible, emphasizing a utilitarian design focused on durability and function. The exterior materials and finishes are typical of traditional rural architecture, blending practicality with a simple, clean aesthetic.
description: A large L-shaped cowshed with a red metal hip roof, beige wooden plank walls, and an open bay supported by posts. The structure features barn-style doors and a robust, utilitarian design.
description_view_0: The front-left view shows the L-shaped layout, red hip roof, two large barn doors, and an open bay supported by posts.
description_view_1: The side view displays the long, uninterrupted beige wooden wall and the overhanging red roof, with no visible doors or windows.
description_view_2: The rear view highlights the full length of the cowshed, showing the intersection of the two roof sections and the enclosed extension.
description_view_3: The top-down view emphasizes the L-shaped footprint and the extensive red hip roof covering both sections.
attribute_door_style: double sliding barn door, wood construction with metal hardware
